_meta.canonical_reference: Schubert T., Friedmann F., Regenbrecht H. (2001). The experience of presence: Factor analytic insights. Presence: Teleoperators and Virtual Environments, 10(3), 266-281.

SP.name: Spatial Presence
SP.description: Sense of physically being in the virtual environment

items.text: I had a sense of acting in the virtual space.
I felt present in the virtual space.
I did not feel present in the virtual space.
I had a sense of being there.
I felt like I was just perceiving pictures.

SP.scoring: Mean of item scores after reversing reverse-coded items. Range 1-7.

INV.name: Involvement
INV.description: Attention and involvement directed toward the virtual environment

items.text: I was not aware of my real environment.
I was completely captivated by the virtual world.
I noticed that my attention was in the virtual environment.

REAL.name: Experienced Realism
REAL.description: Subjective experience of realism in the virtual environment

items.text: The virtual world seemed realistic to me.
How much did the visual aspects of the environment seem real?
How real did the virtual world seem to you?
The virtual environment appeared real to me.

G.name: General Presence

items.text: In the computer generated world I had a sense of 'being there'.

G.scoring: Single item score 1-7. Used as a reference/anchor; not included in composite.

composite_scoring.total_presence_score: Mean of SP, INV, and REAL subscale scores (G excluded). Range 1-7.

administration.timing: Administered immediately after VR exposure ends
